name: Leadco Community
description_short: Leadco Community Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving Missouri residents and employees in specific industries since 1953, offering checking, savings, loans, and mortgage services.
description_long:
Leadco Community Credit Union was established in 1953 to serve employees of the St. Joe Lead Company and its subsidiaries. Over the past seven decades, the organization has expanded its membership beyond its original employee base to serve a broader geographic and occupational community. As a credit union, Leadco operates as a democratic cooperative owned and controlled by its members through an elected Board of Directors.

The organization provides a comprehensive range of consumer banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), individual retirement accounts (IRAs), and various loan products. Leadco offers auto financing for new and used vehicles with competitive rates ranging from 4.24% to 6.74% depending on vehicle year and loan term, RV and utility loans, mortgage solutions through its subsidiary Leadco Mortgage Solutions, and general personal loans. Members can access services through physical branch locations, shared branch networks (100+ locations in Missouri and 4,900+ nationwide), online banking, mobile banking via the TouchBanking app, and a toll-free audio teller line.

Lleadco distinguishes itself through its non-profit cooperative structure, which means earnings are returned to members rather than external shareholders. The organization offers membership to anyone living or working in St. Francois, Madison, or Ste. Genevieve Counties in Missouri, as well as employees in mining, smelting, quarrying, local government, hospitals, schools, and manufacturing across a seven-county region. The mobile banking app includes mobile check deposit functionality for $0.50 per item, and members benefit from skip-a-payment options and low-rate loan programs typical of credit unions.

As a regional credit union, Leadco's primary limitation is geographic and occupational eligibility restrictions that exclude many potential members outside their service areas and employment categories. While they offer competitive rates and member-focused services, their product offerings are more limited compared to large national banks, and their technology infrastructure appears less sophisticated than newer fintech-oriented competitors. For eligible members within their service territory seeking traditional banking services with a cooperative ownership model and competitive rates, Leadco provides a solid local option.
services: Checking and savings accounts with online and mobile access
Auto loans for new and used vehicles with terms up to 84 months
RV and utility loans with rates from 6.99% to 7.49%
Mortgage origination and refinancing through Leadco Mortgage Solutions
Online and mobile banking with bill pay functionality
Mobile check deposit service ($0.50 per item)
Shared branch access and CO-OP network locations nationwide
pros: Non-profit cooperative structure returns earnings to members rather than external shareholders
Competitive auto loan rates: 4.24% for new vehicles (1-36 months) and 4.99% for used vehicles (1-36 months)
Access to 4,900+ shared branch locations nationwide plus 100+ in Missouri for convenient service
Free mobile banking app with mobile check deposit functionality at just $0.50 per item
Skip-a-payment option and flexible loan terms available (up to 84 months for auto loans)
Low-cost online setup and no-fee virtual branch access for existing members
Member-controlled governance through elected volunteer Board of Directors
cons: Restricted membership eligibility based on geographic location (Missouri counties) and employment type limits accessibility
Limited branch network compared to national banks—only one primary location listed (Farmington, MO)
Rates and product features are subject to credit scoring restrictions that may apply based on creditworthiness
No mention of checking account overdraft protection, rewards programs, or premium account tiers typical of modern banks
Limited technology infrastructure—mobile app requires Access Code entry and appears less user-friendly than competitors
best_for: Employees in mining, smelting, manufacturing, or government sectors in the specified Missouri counties seeking personalized service
Residents of St. Francois, Madison, or Ste. Genevieve Counties wanting a locally-owned financial institution with competitive rates
Auto borrowers with good credit seeking below-market interest rates on vehicle financing
Members prioritizing community reinvestment and cooperative ownership over corporate banking structures
diagnosis: Leadco Community Credit Union is best suited for Missouri residents and eligible employees in specific industries seeking traditional banking services with competitive loan rates and member-owned cooperative benefits. The primary caveat is membership eligibility—potential customers must live or work in designated Missouri counties or qualifying employment sectors, making this option unavailable for the general population outside these geographic and occupational parameters.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
